transcript.text: I’ve confirmed this is a fairly small app, which is helpful: the real runtime seems to be driven by one provider, CSS imports, a fetch hook, and a few browser APIs like `localStorage`, `reload`, `share`, and clipboard. I’m reading those files now so we can copy the real patterns precisely into the shared Storybook environment instead of scattering setup into stories.

transcript.text: I’ve got the main runtime shape now: `main.tsx` imports `index.css`, wraps `App` in `LikedArticlesProvider`, and the live app relies on `localStorage`, `fetch`, `Image` preloading, `IntersectionObserver`, `window.location.reload`, `navigator.share`, `navigator.clipboard`, `alert`, and a download click path. I’m checking the remaining `App` JSX and the current Storybook files next so I can replace the starter setup cleanly and only add global mocks the real app actually needs.

transcript.text: The app doesn’t use React portals, so I’m not planning a `preview-body.html` unless I discover one during verification. The main Storybook work now is: copy the real provider and CSS into preview, add stable globals for the fetch and browser APIs the app actually touches, then replace the starter stories with a small set of thin stories around `WikiCard`, `LanguageSelector`, and `App`.

I’m at the edit phase now. I’m replacing the starter Storybook preview with a single app-shaped environment, removing the boilerplate sample stories so only real app patterns remain, and adding a few very thin stories that mostly reuse the real JSX shapes from `App.tsx`.

The Storybook defaults and the first pass of thin stories are in place. I’m typechecking now before I run the browser-based story tests, because preview and story typing tends to catch the fastest feedback on these setups.

transcript.command: /bin/zsh -lc "printf '\\n--- preview.tsx ---\\n'; sed -n '1,260p' .storybook/preview.tsx; printf '\\n--- App.stories.tsx ---\\n'; sed -n '1,220p' src/App.stories.tsx; printf '\\n--- LanguageSelector.stories.tsx ---\\n'; sed -n '1,220p' src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx; printf '\\n--- WikiCard.stories.tsx ---\\n'; sed -n '1,220p' src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx"

transcript.text: Typecheck is clean, so I’m moving to the browser-backed story tests now. This should surface whether the global preview mocks are actually strong enough for the real app patterns, especially the fetch path and the interactive overlays.

The story tests are running in the browser now. I’m watching for interaction failures and visual setup issues first; if anything breaks, I’ll tighten the shared preview rather than patching individual stories.

transcript.text:
Storybook now mirrors the real app runtime instead of the starter boilerplate. The shared setup in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real `LikedArticlesProvider`, sets default `localStorage`, and globally mocks the actual side effects the app uses: Wikipedia `fetch`, `navigator.share`, clipboard writes, `window.location.reload`, `alert`, download clicks, and `sendBeacon`. I also added shared deterministic wiki data in [.storybook/wikiMockData.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/wikiMockData.ts), updated [.storybook/main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts) to serve `public/` and drop the unused starter MDX glob, and included Storybook files in [tsconfig.app.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json) so preview typing is checked too.

The stories are thin and copied from real usage patterns: [src/App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx), and [src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx). I removed the unrelated starter Storybook sample stories under `src/stories/`. I did not add `preview-body.html` because this app does not use portals.

Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx`; both passed. I left the unrelated untracked eval artifact [.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md) untouched.

items.text: Replace boilerplate Storybook config with app-matching preview defaults and supporting mocks
Add thin colocated stories copied from real component and page usage patterns
Run typecheck and Storybook Vitest screenshot checks, then fix any issues

content.text: I’m tracing the real app runtime first so the Storybook setup copies the actual environment instead of inventing one. I’ll map providers, CSS, browser APIs, fetches, and portal usage, then I’ll wire those defaults into `.storybook/preview.tsx` and keep the stories as thin as possible.

content.text: The app doesn’t use React portals, so I’m not planning a `preview-body.html` unless I discover one during verification. The main Storybook work now is: copy the real provider and CSS into preview, add stable globals for the fetch and browser APIs the app actually touches, then replace the starter stories with a small set of thin stories around `WikiCard`, `LanguageSelector`, and `App`.

I’m at the edit phase now. I’m replacing the starter Storybook preview with a single app-shaped environment, removing the boilerplate sample stories so only real app patterns remain, and adding a few very thin stories that mostly reuse the real JSX shapes from `App.tsx`.

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the real app runtime instead of the starter boilerplate. The shared setup in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real `LikedArticlesProvider`, sets default `localStorage`, and globally mocks the actual side effects the app uses: Wikipedia `fetch`, `navigator.share`, clipboard writes, `window.location.reload`, `alert`, download clicks, and `sendBeacon`. I also added shared deterministic wiki data in [.storybook/wikiMockData.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/wikiMockData.ts), updated [.storybook/main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts) to serve `public/` and drop the unused starter MDX glob, and included Storybook files in [tsconfig.app.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json) so preview typing is checked too.

The stories are thin and copied from real usage patterns: [src/App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx), and [src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx). I removed the unrelated starter Storybook sample stories under `src/stories/`. I did not add `preview-body.html` because this app does not use portals.

Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx`; both passed. I left the unrelated untracked eval artifact [.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-d809c506/project/frontend/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md) untouched.
